content:
3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Markus Winkler on UnsplashSecure coding should always be the top priority for developers while building applications.
Hence it was a welcome news when the HTTP QUERY Method was formally introduced by IETF (Internet Engineering Task Force) in RFC 10008 last month i.e. June 2026.
While building REST APIs or integrating external endpoints, I would always wonder why does GET method expose all query parameters in the URI itself?
And URI rate limitations, restricts the usage of GET method for complex long character queries.
The obvious alternative is POST method, however it was naturally designed for operations that can create, update or modify resources on the server.
The QUERY method bridges exactly this gap where it is intended to perform query searches both simple and complex in the request body. It makes the intent clearer, safer and allows for reliable retries.
Additionally with QUERY method comes the optional Accept-Query header if you want to tell the server which query format you support (i.e. CQL JSON, GraphQL, other structured query formats).
Major platforms like Spring Boot, FastAPI, ASP.NET Core, Node.js (Express), Go, NGINX, and Apache have begun adding support for QUERY method.
